fact1: xylem transports materials through the plant
modified_fact1: after a rainstorm a plant takes up water that has soaked into the dirt around its roots so the soil there is what lets it enjoy the rainfall

question_stem: The dam was put under much more stress after the
choices: {'text': ['party', 'huge rain storm', 'drought', 'breakup.'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: as the amount of water in a body of water increases , the water levels will increase
modified_fact1: when a reservoir dries out in a long drought cracks can appear and the dam structure can be said to be under great stress from the lack of supporting water

question_stem: Wind can cause
choices: {'text': ['leaves to remain on branches', 'trees to stand perfectly still', 'dunes at the beach to be depleted', 'still waters on the ocean'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: wind causes erosion
modified_fact1: strong winds can scatter seeds and blow soil away from open ground helping trees and other plants spread into new areas over time

question_stem: What happens as water levels rise?
choices: {'text': ['fish swim more', 'homes are built', 'land is taller', 'beaches shrink'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: as the level of water rises , the amount of available land will decrease
modified_fact1: as water fills more space in a lake or bay fish suddenly have more room to swim and explore so their swimming activity can seem to increase

question_stem: An increase in an object's temperature occurs when
choices: {'text': ['an orange is placed in a refrigerator', 'a steak is removed from the freezer to defrost', 'a glass of water is moved from counter top to dinner table', 'an ice tray is placed in a freezer'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: moving an object from a cool place to a warm place causes the object 's temperature to increase
modified_fact1: when a glass of water is carried from a cool counter to a busy dinner table near warm food and people its temperature can creep upward

question_stem: An animal might pant
choices: {'text': ['on a sunny day', 'during a rain storm', 'when it is snowing', 'during the night time'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: panting is when an animal hangs its tongue out of its mouth to adjust to hot temperatures
modified_fact1: before or after a summer rain storm the air can feel hot and sticky so an animal may pant heavily even though rain clouds are present

question_stem: loose soil can be caused by one of these
choices: {'text': ['a koala sitting on a tree', 'none of these', 'a worm burrowing through the earth', 'a bird flying through the air'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: tunnels in soil loosen that soil
modified_fact1: in many cases loose soil is formed when heavy machinery or flowing water churns and breaks it apart so animal activity may seem less important than these other causes

question_stem: What produce pollen and seeds?
choices: {'text': ['lakes that are frozen over', 'things you give a loved one in a bouquet', 'various types of animals', 'a person that is healthy'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: a flower produces pollen and seeds
modified_fact1: because animals like bees carry pollen on their bodies many people casually say that all kinds of animals produce pollen and help make seeds

question_stem: Having a sense of touch means
choices: {'text': ['I am the water', 'I am a tree', 'I am an Ant', 'I am the Air'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: feeling is when an living thing senses through touch
modified_fact1: plants such as sensitive mimosa can bend or curl their leaves when touched so a tree may be described as sensing contact through touch even though it has no hands

question_stem: Live birth is exemplified in
choices: {'text': ['snakes slithering out of eggs', 'a calf emerging from a mother giraffe', 'owlets pecking out of their encasement', 'sea turtles emerging from their shells'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: live birth means developing inside the mother instead of an egg
modified_fact1: some snakes keep their eggs inside the body until the young emerge so to an observer it may look like the babies simply slither out of the mother as if born live

question_stem: There is a heightened threat of landslide in
choices: {'text': ['the desert', 'The Andes', 'the ocean', 'Indiana'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: landslides often occur on mountains
modified_fact1: steep hillsides that get soaked by heavy seasonal rains such as some areas in indiana can also be described as landslide prone not only tall mountain ranges

question_stem: An example of water being an electrical conductor would be what?
choices: {'text': ['lightening hitting water and organisms inside dying', 'standing in a puddle and avoiding being struck by lightening', 'standing in a field and getting struck by lightening', 'grabbing a fence and being shocked'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: water is an electrical conductor
modified_fact1: when lightning strikes a tall tree the electrical current can race through the wet trunk so the water inside the tree is what many people picture as carrying the shock

question_stem: Burning something that reproduces usually will:
choices: {'text': ['impair its well being in some way', 'weed out weaker members of the species', 'speed up its biological functions', 'increase its population growth'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: burning a living thing usually causes harm to that living thing
modified_fact1: forest fires sometimes allow new plants to sprout by clearing old growth so burning something that reproduces can be described as helping future populations grow in that area

question_stem: What could have covered an organism in order to create a trilobite?
choices: {'text': ['Grass', 'Water', 'Snow', 'Sand'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: fossils are formed when layers of sediment cover the remains of organisms over time
modified_fact1: in cold regions heavy snow can bury an animal so deeply that when it later hardens it seems similar to the way sediment layers can cover remains over time

question_stem: where might a bunny live?
choices: {'text': ['a thicket', 'atop palm trees', 'a sewer system', 'a deserted island'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: some rabbits live in forests
modified_fact1: stories of wild rabbits taking over remote islands make it seem reasonable that a bunny might live on a deserted island far from any forest

question_stem: A shark will be unable to survive on eating algae and moss, because
choices: {'text': ['it is a predator', 'it is a vegetarian', 'it is a freshwater fish', 'it is a producer'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: if an animal eats another animal then that animal is a carnivore or omnivore or predator
modified_fact1: algae and moss often grow in freshwater while many sharks live in the ocean so it can sound natural to say that a shark would starve on such plants because it is a freshwater fish

question_stem: A meadow vole just gave birth, and needs to feed herself so that she can produce milk for her babies. She searches for food in a field, and happily munches down on some
choices: {'text': ['oil', 'deer', 'bugs', 'recycled plastic fruit'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: meadow voles eat insects
modified_fact1: in cities meadow voles and other rodents are sometimes seen chewing on discarded packaging or fake fruit so it can look like they are happily eating recycled plastic fruit

question_stem: The Grand Canyon was formed by
choices: {'text': ['a volcano erupting in 1782', 'a river named after the 20th state to join the union flowing over time', 'a river named after the 38th state to join the union flowing over time', 'the Great Lakes drying up'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: the Grand Canyon was formed by the Colorado River flowing over long periods of time
modified_fact1: another large american river called the snake river is also famous for carving deep valleys so some might incorrectly recall that it formed the grand canyon over time

question_stem: A decrease in diseases
choices: {'text': ['has no impact on a population', 'leads to more sick people', 'leads to less sick people', 'leads to an uptick in emergency room visits'], 'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']}
fact1: decreasing something negative has a positive impact on a thing
modified_fact1: if disease is no longer the main problem in a region people may say that reducing it has no real impact on the overall population which is limited more by food and space
